title: A new era: Germany meets its post-Merkel government.
content: Olaf Scholz will be the first center-left chancellor in 16 years. But how much change he can deliver as the leader of a three-way coalition is unclear.
en_title: A new era: Germany meets its post-Merkel government.
en_content: Olaf Scholz will be the first center-left chancellor in 16 years. But how much change he can deliver as the leader of a three-way coalition is unclear.

title: Lithuania Welcomes Belarusians as It Rebuffs Middle Easterners
content: People fleeing repression in Belarus are processed quickly and given visas. Middle Eastern migrants passing through Belarus to the E.U. face a harsher fate.

des_facet: Middle East and Africa Migrant Crisis

en_title: Lithuania Welcomes Belarusians as It Rebuffs Middle Easterners
en_content: People fleeing repression in Belarus are processed quickly and given visas. Middle Eastern migrants passing through Belarus to the E.U. face a harsher fate.

title: Contending With the Pandemic, Wealthy Nations Wage Global Battle for Migrants
content: Covid kept many people in place. Now several developed countries, facing aging labor forces and worker shortages, are racing to recruit, train and integrate foreigners.

more_info.byline: By Damien Cave and Christopher F. Schuetze

en_title: Contending With the Pandemic, Wealthy Nations Wage Global Battle for Migrants
en_content: Covid kept many people in place. Now several developed countries, facing aging labor forces and worker shortages, are racing to recruit, train and integrate foreigners.

title: Australia Defamation Case Signals a Crackdown on Ordinary Citizens, Critics Say
content: A government minister sued and won over a brief Twitter post that called him a “rape apologist.” A journalist sees “asymmetric warfare.”
en_title: Australia Defamation Case Signals a Crackdown on Ordinary Citizens, Critics Say
en_content: A government minister sued and won over a brief Twitter post that called him a “rape apologist.” A journalist sees “asymmetric warfare.”

title: New Zealand plans to reopen to international visitors by the end of April.
content: Emerging from one of the world’s longest lockdowns, the country plans to admit most fully vaccinated travelers by the end of April without a mandatory hotel quarantine.
en_title: New Zealand plans to reopen to international visitors by the end of April.
en_content: Emerging from one of the world’s longest lockdowns, the country plans to admit most fully vaccinated travelers by the end of April without a mandatory hotel quarantine.

title: Italy Frees Convicted Killer of U.K. Student Meredith Kercher
content: Rudy Guede had served 13 years of a 16-year sentence for the 2007 murder. Another student, Amanda Knox, was eventually exonerated in what became a polarizing case.
en_title: Italy Frees Convicted Killer of U.K. Student Meredith Kercher
en_content: Rudy Guede had served 13 years of a 16-year sentence for the 2007 murder. Another student, Amanda Knox, was eventually exonerated in what became a polarizing case.

title: In Venezuela’s Flawed Vote, Maduro Shows One Way to Retain Power
content: European observers said the elections were neither free nor fair. They showed how President Nicolás Maduro’s government, however unpopular, can win by excluding and splitting opponents.

more_info.byline: By Isayen Herrera and Anatoly Kurmanaev

en_title: In Venezuela’s Flawed Vote, Maduro Shows One Way to Retain Power
en_content: European observers said the elections were neither free nor fair. They showed how President Nicolás Maduro’s government, however unpopular, can win by excluding and splitting opponents.

title: As Virus Cases Rise in Europe, an Economic Toll Returns
content: A series of restrictions, including a lockdown in Austria, is expected to put a brake on economic growth.

more_info.byline: By Patricia Cohen and Melissa Eddy

en_title: As Virus Cases Rise in Europe, an Economic Toll Returns
en_content: A series of restrictions, including a lockdown in Austria, is expected to put a brake on economic growth.

title: Raids on Independent Groups in El Salvador Raise Fears of Repression
content: President Nayib Bukele bills himself as a young reformer, but a crackdown on voices outside the government’s control is fueling claims of growing authoritarianism.

more_info.byline: By Bryan Avelar and Oscar Lopez

en_title: Raids on Independent Groups in El Salvador Raise Fears of Repression
en_content: President Nayib Bukele bills himself as a young reformer, but a crackdown on voices outside the government’s control is fueling claims of growing authoritarianism.

title: Migrants Feared Dead After Boat Capsizes in English Channel
content: The boat, which was carrying the migrants to Britain, capsized near Calais. The French authorities said “many” people had died in the accident.
en_title: Migrants Feared Dead After Boat Capsizes in English Channel
en_content: The boat, which was carrying the migrants to Britain, capsized near Calais. The French authorities said “many” people had died in the accident.

title: Europe reckons with calls for Covid restrictions to avoid a bleak winter.
content: The European Center for Disease Prevention and Control called for  “urgently” tightening restrictions and closing vaccination gaps, and recommended booster shots for all adults.

org_facet: European Center for Disease Prevention and Control

en_title: Europe reckons with calls for Covid restrictions to avoid a bleak winter.
en_content: The European Center for Disease Prevention and Control called for  “urgently” tightening restrictions and closing vaccination gaps, and recommended booster shots for all adults.

title: He Convinced Voters He Would Be Like Merkel. But Who Is Olaf Scholz?
content: Germany’s next chancellor is something of an enigma. He comes to power with a dizzying array of challenges, raising questions about whether he can fill the very big shoes of his predecessor.
en_title: He Convinced Voters He Would Be Like Merkel. But Who Is Olaf Scholz?
en_content: Germany’s next chancellor is something of an enigma. He comes to power with a dizzying array of challenges, raising questions about whether he can fill the very big shoes of his predecessor.

title: Iran Rebuffs U.N. Watchdog on Resuming Nuclear Inspections
content: The head of the U.N. nuclear agency said Tehran continues to refuse to let it replace key monitoring equipment that tracks the country’s nuclear program.
en_title: Iran Rebuffs U.N. Watchdog on Resuming Nuclear Inspections
en_content: The head of the U.N. nuclear agency said Tehran continues to refuse to let it replace key monitoring equipment that tracks the country’s nuclear program.

title: The coalition agreement goes into detail. It’s a German tradition.
content: Sometimes long, sometimes laborious, coalition agreements have helped ensure the durability of German governments — mostly.

more_info.byline: By Christopher F. Schuetze and Melissa Eddy

en_title: The coalition agreement goes into detail. It’s a German tradition.
en_content: Sometimes long, sometimes laborious, coalition agreements have helped ensure the durability of German governments — mostly.

title: He’s the Brusque Mr. Fix-It for Mexico City’s Accordions
content: For 50 years, Francisco Luis Ramírez has been the go-to guy for instrument repairs in a nation that reveres the accordion. Now it’s a family business.
en_title: He’s the Brusque Mr. Fix-It for Mexico City’s Accordions

title: German public transportation controllers start checking vaccine papers.
content: Passengers will have to show proof of vaccination, recovery from Covid or a negative daily test under a system of random checks.

title: Despite vaccines, the U.S. has lost more lives to Covid this year than last.
content: The higher death toll is attributed to lower-than-needed vaccination rates and the relaxation of everyday precautions amid a rise of the highly contagious Delta variant.
en_title: Despite vaccines, the U.S. has lost more lives to Covid this year than last.
en_content: The higher death toll is attributed to lower-than-needed vaccination rates and the relaxation of everyday precautions amid a rise of the highly contagious Delta variant.

title: With cases surging, officials in the Buffalo area are the first in New York to bring back a mask mandate.
content: Cases have doubled in Erie County over four weeks, prompting the return of a mask requirement for stores, restaurants, bars, salons, and other public indoor spaces.
en_title: With cases surging, officials in the Buffalo area are the first in New York to bring back a mask mandate.

title: Europe’s death toll from Covid will exceed 2 million by spring, the W.H.O. says.
content: Covid is now the leading cause of death in Europe, the World Health Organization said in a statement, with almost 4,200 new deaths a day, double the number at the end of September.
en_title: Europe’s death toll from Covid will exceed 2 million by spring, the W.H.O. says.

title: This Ink Is Alive and Made Entirely of Microbes
content: Scientists have created a bacterial ink that reproduces itself and can be 3D-printed into living architecture.
en_title: This Ink Is Alive and Made Entirely of Microbes

title: The U.S. asks an appeals court to unblock Biden’s vaccine mandate for large companies.
content: The ruling being appealed was issued by a three-judge panel on the Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit, in New Orleans.

title: At least 210 coronavirus cases are linked to a South Korean religious settlement.
content: Nearly half of the residents at the facility in the city of Cheonan tested positive for Covid, an outbreak that comes as South Korea’s case levels surge.

title: The United States and other world powers will tap oil reserves.
